briefdescription.para: These constants specify different types of handler and used to differentiate interrupt requests from peripheral.
This enumeration defines the possible states of the HDMI transmitter stream.

detaileddescription.para: It is used to indicate whether the HDMI stream is currently active or inactive.

briefdescription.para: This enumeration specifies the different audio formats supported by the HDMI transmitter.
This enumeration lists the various SCDC fields used in HDMI 2.0/2.1 communication between the HDMI transmitter and receiver.

detaileddescription.para: These fields are used for configuring and monitoring the FRL (Fixed Rate Link) and other HDMI link parameters.

sectiondef.header: HDMI TX CTS and N Source

briefdescription.para: This enumeration defines whether the HDMI transmitter uses external or internal sources for the CTS (Clock Time Stamp) and N values, which are used in audio clock recovery.
Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
This macro returns the clock cycles required to count up to 1MS with respect to AXI Lite Frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTX1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the clock cycles required to count up to 10MS with respect to AXI Lite Frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTX core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the TX version.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTX1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro asserts or releases the HDMI TX reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI TX reset.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI . Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI link and video reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_Reset(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This macro controls the HDMI TX Scrambler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either set ON or clear Scrambler.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_SetScrambler(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro controls the YUV420 mode for video bridge.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Tx core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the YUV 420 Support.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_Bridge_yuv420(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro controls the Pixel Repeat mode for video bridge.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Tx core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the Pixel Repetition Support.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_Bridge_pixel(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI TX PIO peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI TX PIO peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupt in the HDMI TX PIO peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupt in the HDMI TX PIO peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro clears HDMI TX PIO interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI TX Display Data Channel (DDC) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI TX Display Data Channel (DDC) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupt in the HDMI TX DDC peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupt in the HDMI TX DDC peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro clears HDMI TX DDC interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI TX Auxiliary (AUX) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupt in the HDMI TX AUX peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro allows enabling/disabling of VRR in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the VFP Event

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_VrrControl(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro allows enabling/disabling of FSync in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the VFP Event

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_FSyncControl(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the data mover for Dynamic HDR in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the data mover for Dynamic HDR in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro allows enabling/disabling of Dynamic HDR in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the Dynamic HDR

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_DynHdrControl(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro allows enabling/disabling of FAPA Location Dynamic HDR in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value for FAPA location.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_DynHdr_FAPA_Control(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro allows enabling/disabling of GOF Dynamic HDR in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the GOF (Graphics Overlay Flag)

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_DynHdr_GOF_Control(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro allows set/clear of GOF Value Dynamic HDR in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either set or clear the GOF (Graphics Overlay Flag) Value

memberdef.name: XV_HdmiTx1_DynHdr_MTW_Clear
memberdef.initializer: { \ u32 val; \ if (InstancePtr->Config.DynHdr) { \ val = XV_HdmiTx1_ReadReg((InstancePtr)->Config.BaseAddress, \ XV_HDMITX1_AUX_STA_OFFSET); \ val |= XV_HDMITX1_AUX_STA_DYNHDR_MTW_MASK; \ XV_HdmiTx1_WriteReg((InstancePtr)->Config.BaseAddress, \ (XV_HDMITX1_AUX_STA_OFFSET), \ val); \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: This macro allows to set the MTW bit to clear it for Dynamic HDR in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro gets the read status of Data Mover for Dynamic HDR in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: u32 val - 0 means ok else memory read error

memberdef.name: XV_HdmiTx1_DynHdr_SetPacket
memberdef.initializer: { \ if (InstancePtr->Config.DynHdr) { \ u32 val = (u16)PktType | \ ((u16)PktLen << XV_HDMITX1_AUX_DYNHDR_PKT_LENGTH_SHIFT); \ XV_HdmiTx1_WriteReg((InstancePtr)->Config.BaseAddress, \ XV_HDMITX1_AUX_DYNHDR_PKT_OFFSET, \ val); \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the Header packet type and length for Dynamic HDR in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
is a u16 length of Dynamic HDR packet.
is a u16 Type of Dynamic HDR packet.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_DynHdr_SetPacket(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u16 PktLen, u16 PktType)

memberdef.name: XV_HdmiTx1_DynHdr_SetAddr
memberdef.initializer: { \ if (InstancePtr->Config.DynHdr) { \ u32 val; \ val = (u32)Addr; \ XV_HdmiTx1_WriteReg((InstancePtr)->Config.BaseAddress, \ XV_HDMITX1_AUX_DYNHDR_ADDR_LSB_OFFSET, \ val); \ val = (u32)(((u64)Addr & 0xFFFFFFFF00000000) >> 32); \ XV_HdmiTx1_WriteReg((InstancePtr)->Config.BaseAddress, \ XV_HDMITX1_AUX_DYNHDR_ADDR_MSB_OFFSET, \ val); \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the buffer address for Dynamic HDR in HDMI Tx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
is a u64 which contains the buffer address

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_DynHdr_SetAddr(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u64 Addr)

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupt in the HDMI TX AUX peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables audio in HDMI TX core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the mode bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro clears the mode bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro provides the current mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro provides the current sample rate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro provides the active audio channels.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro provides the current pixel packing phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables video mask in HDMI TX core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables video mask in HDMI TX core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables or disables the noise in the video mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Tx core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the Noise.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_MaskNoise(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the red component value in the video mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_MaskSetRed(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u16 Value)

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the green component value in the video mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_MaskSetGreen(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u16 Value)

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the blue component value in the video mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_MaskSetBlue(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u16 Value)

briefdescription.para: This macro provides the current video mask mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: Current mode. 0 = Video masking is disabled 1 = Video masking is enabled

briefdescription.para: This macro allows enabling/disabling of DSC in HDMI-TX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the DSC

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_DscControl(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: Callback type for Vsync event interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a reference to an XV_HdmiTx1_Config structure based on the core id, DeviceId.

detaileddescription.para: The return value will refer to an entry in the device configuration table defined in the xv_hdmitx1_g.c file.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique core ID of the HDMI TX core for the lookup operation.

simplesect.para: XV_HdmiTx1_LookupConfig returns a reference to a config record in the configuration table (in xv_hdmitx1_g.c) corresponding to DeviceId, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the HDMI TX core.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the HDMI TX core. Initialization of the HDMI TX includes setting up the instance data and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
points to the configuration structure associated with the HDMI TX core.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if HDMI TX PIO ID mismatched.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the core into HDMI TMDS mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: This is required after a reset or init.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the core into DVI mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: This is required after a reset or init.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the HDMI TX Auxiliary (AUX) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables audio in HDMI TX core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function clear the HDMI TX variables and sets it to the defaults.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: This is required after a reset or init.

briefdescription.para: This function provides video identification code of video mode.

parameterdescription.para: specifies resolution identifier.

simplesect.para: Video identification code defined in the VIC table.

briefdescription.para: Get video mode from VIC (Video Identification Code).

parameterdescription.para: is the Video Identification Code.

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, XVidC_VideoTiming VideoTiming, XVidC_FrameRate FrameRate, XVidC_ColorFormat ColorFormat, XVidC_ColorDepth Bpc, XVidC_PixelsPerClock Ppc, XVidC_3DInfo *Info3D, u8 FVaFactor, u8 VrrEnabled, u8 CnmvrrEnabled, u64 *TmdsClk)
memberdef.name: XV_HdmiTx1_SetStream

briefdescription.para: This function sets the HDMI TX stream parameters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies video timing.
specifies frame rate.

specifies the type of color format.
0 = XVIDC_CSF_RGB
1 = XVIDC_CSF_YCRCB_444
2 = XVIDC_CSF_YCRCB_422
3 = XVIDC_CSF_YCRCB_420

specifies the color depth/bits per color component.
6 = XVIDC_BPC_6
8 = XVIDC_BPC_8
10 = XVIDC_BPC_10
12 = XVIDC_BPC_12
16 = XVIDC_BPC_16

specifies the pixel per clock.
4 = XVIDC_PPC_4
3D info
- Fast Video Active Factor
- VRR is enabled or not
- Negative VRR supported flag
- reference clock calculated based on the input parameters.

inbodydescription.para: In HDMI the colordepth in YUV422 is always 12 bits, although on the link itself it is being transmitted as 8-bits. Therefore if the colorspace is YUV422, then force the colordepth to 12 bits.

briefdescription.para: This function sets and return the TMDS Clock based on Video Parameter from the InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI TX Internal VRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI TX Internal VRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI TX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI Internal video reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_INT_VRST(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI TX Internal LRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI TX Internal LRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI TX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI Internal link reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_INT_VRST(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI TX External VRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI TX External VRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI TX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI external video reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_EXT_VRST(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI TX External SYSRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI TX External SYSRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI TX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI External system reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiTx1_EXT_SYSRST(XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function sets the HDMI TX AUX GCP register AVMUTE bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the HDMI TX AUX GCP register AVMUTE bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the HDMI TX AUX GCP register CLEAR_AVMUTE bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the HDMI TX AUX GCP register CLEAR_AVMUTE bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the pixel rate at output.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the sample rate at output.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

specifies the value that needs to be set.
2 samples per clock
3 samples per clock.
5 samples per clock.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the color format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the color depth.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function provides status of the stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function provides the stream connected status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the AXI4-Lite Clock Frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies the value that needs to be set.

simplesect.para: This is required after a reset or init.

briefdescription.para: This function prepares TX DDC peripheral to use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies the value that needs to be set.

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Slave, u16 Length, u8 *Buffer, u8 Stop)
memberdef.name: XV_HdmiTx1_DdcWrite

briefdescription.para: This function writes data from DDC peripheral from given slave address.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies the slave address from where data needs to be read.
specifies number of bytes to be read.
specifies a pointer to u8 variable that will be filled with data.
specifies the stop flag which is either TRUE/FALSE.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if an acknowledgement received and timeout.

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Slave, u16 Length, u8 *Buffer, u8 Stop)
memberdef.name: XV_HdmiTx1_DdcRead

briefdescription.para: This function reads data from DDC peripheral from given slave address.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies the slave address from where data needs to be read.
specifies number of bytes to be read.
specifies a pointer to u8 variable that will be filled with data.
specifies the stop flag which is either TRUE/FALSE.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if an acknowledgement received and timeout.

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_HdmiTx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Slave, u16 Length, u8 RegAddr, u8 *Buffer)
memberdef.name: XV_HdmiTx1_DdcReadReg

briefdescription.para: This function reads specified register from DDC peripheral from given slave address.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies the slave address from where data needs to be read.
specifies number of bytes to be read.
specifies the register address from where data needs to be read.
specifies a pointer to u8 variable that will be filled with data.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if an acknowledgement received and timeout.

briefdescription.para: This function writes the specified SCDC Field.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies the fields from SCDC channels to be written
specifies the values to be written

briefdescription.para: This function transmits the DSC packet header.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
value to be written

briefdescription.para: This function transmits the DSC packet data.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
value to be written

briefdescription.para: This function transmits the infoframes generated by the processor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if AUX FIFO is full.

briefdescription.para: This function controls the scrambler.

detaileddescription.para: Requires TMDSClock to be up to date in order to force enable scrambler when TMDSClock > 340MHz.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function controls the TMDS clock ratio.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function detects connected sink is a HDMI 2.0/HDMI 1.4 sink device and sets appropriate flag in the TX stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function shows the sinks SCDC registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints stream and timing information on STDIO/Uart console.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints debug information on STDIO/UART console.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints out HDMI TX register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the active audio channels.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
is number of active audio channels

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if active channels were set.
XST_FAILURE if no active channels were set.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the active audio format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.
specifies the audio format

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if active channels were set.
XST_FAILURE if no active channels were set.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the active audio format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: Active audio format of HDMI Tx

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Generated ACR CTS Value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTxSs1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the programmed ACR N Value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTxSs1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function Starts the internal ACR module for TMDS.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: Start TMDS mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS on success, error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the CED and RSED registers from the sink and returns the pointer to the data structure which stores the CED related readings (from SCDC register 0x50 to 0x5A).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: Returns a pointer to the local buffer containing the SCDC character error detection readings
Reading the SCDC registers will clear the values at the sink

briefdescription.para: This function starts the HDMI TX core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: This is required after a reset or initialization.

briefdescription.para: This function stops the HDMI TX core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads ID of HDMI TX PIO peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if PIO ID was matched.
XST_FAILURE if PIO ID was mismatched.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the HDMI TX driver.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the pending interrupt from PIO and DDC peripheral, determines the source of the interrupts, clears the interrupts and calls callbacks accordingly.

The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XV_HdmiTx1_SetCallback() during initialization phase. An example delivered with this driver demonstrates how this could be done.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiTx1 instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- (XV_HDMITX1_HANDLER_HPD) HpdCallback (XV_HDMITX1_HANDLER_VS) VsCallback

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI TX core instance.
specifies the type of handler.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Video Timing Extended Metadata.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pointer to the SrcProdDescIF which contains the Source Product Descriptor InfoFrame data used in HDMI VRR (Variable Refresh Rate) or other auxiliary metadata transmission.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiTx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function configures the HDMI transmitter with extended video timing metadata such as QMS (Quick Media Switching), VRR (Variable Refresh Rate), and other timing-related parameters.

detaileddescription.para: It prepares the VTEM data based on the provided metadata structure and writes it to the appropriate auxiliary registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiTx1 core instance.
is a pointer to the structure containing extended video timing metadata.

briefdescription.para: This function allows fine-grained control over the VTEM InfoFrame generation by accepting custom values for the sync enable and dataset length fields.

detaileddescription.para: It is useful for debugging or advanced HDMI configurations where default behavior needs to be overridden.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiTx1 core instance.
is a pointer to the structure containing extended video timing metadata.
Value to set the Sync Enable bit (0 or 1).
Value to set the Dataset Length Enable bit (0 or 1).

briefdescription.para: This function configures the HDMI transmitter with Source Product Descriptor metadata, including FreeSync and FreeSync Pro capabilities.

detaileddescription.para: It prepares the data based on the provided structure and writes it to the appropriate auxiliary registers

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiTx1 core instance.
is a pointer to the structure containing SPD InfoFrame data.

briefdescription.para: This is the main header file for Xilinx HDMI TX core.

detaileddescription.para:
HDMI TX core is used for transmitting the incoming video and audio streams. It consists of
Transmitter core
AXI4-Stream to Video Bridge
Video Timing Controller and
High-bandwidth Digital Content Protection (HDCP) (Optional).

The HDMI TX uses three AXI interfaces for Video, Audio and Processor:
AXI4-Stream interface for Video, can be single, dual or quad pixels per clock and supports 8 and 10 bits per component.
AXI4-Stream interface for Audio, accepts multiple channels uncompressed and compressed audio data.
AXI4-Lite interface for processor, controls the transmitter. Please do refer AXI Reference Guide (UG761) for more information on AXI interfaces.

Transmitter core performs following operations:
Converts video data from the video clock domain into the link clock domain.
TMDS (Transition Minimized Differential Signaling) encoding.
Merges encoded video data and packet data into a single HDMI stream.
Optional HDMI stream is encrypted by an external HDCP module.
Over samples HDMI stream if stream bandwidth is too low for the transceiver to handle.
Scrambles encrypted/HDMI stream if data rate is above 3.4 Gbps otherwise bypasses the Scrambler.
AXI Video Bridge converts the incoming video AXI-stream to native video.
Video Timing Controller (VTC) generates the native video timing.
For a full description of HDMI TX features, please see the hardware specification.
The application needs to do following steps in order for preparing the HDMI TX core to be ready.

listitem.para: Call XV_HdmiTx1_LookupConfig using a device ID to find the core configuration.
Call XV_HdmiTx1_CfgInitialize to initialize the device and the driver instance associated with it.

detaileddescription.para:
This driver provides interrupt handlers
XV_HdmiTx1_IntrHandler, for handling the interrupts from the HDMI TX core PIO and DDC peripheral respectively.

Application developer needs to register interrupt handler with the processor, within their examples. Whenever processor calls registered application's interrupt handler associated with interrupt id, application's interrupt handler needs to call appropriate peripheral interrupt handler reading peripheral's Status register.
This driver provides XV_HdmiTx1_SetCallback API to register functions with HDMI TX core instance.
This driver supports Virtual Memory. The RTOS is responsible for calculating the correct device base address in Virtual Memory space.
This driver is not thread safe. Any needs for threads or thread mutual exclusion must be satisfied by the layer above this driver.

Asserts are used within all Xilinx drivers to enforce constraints on argument values. Asserts can be turned off on a system-wide basis by defining at compile time, the NDEBUG identifier. By default, asserts are turned on and it is recommended that users leave asserts on during development.
The HDMI TX driver is composed of several source files. This allows the user to build and link only those parts of the driver that are necessary.

para.preformatted:
MODIFICATION HISTORY: s Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 EB 22/05/18 Initial release.
